subject: mechanism:transport
quote: An epidemiological approach is adopted to develop a model of viral meme propagation. The successful implementation in the modelling of meme spread as reflected in Internet search data shows that memes may be treated as infectious entities when modelling their propagation over time and across societies.
rationale: Paper provides formal SIR-based mathematical apparatus for the transport-side dynamics of meme spread — operationalizing transport as infection.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: Viral memes, in particular, exhibit a characteristic spike early in the infectious stage, followed by a gradual decay of the number of infected people. This behavior is very similar to the dynamics of the classical SIR model
rationale: Paper models viral spread mathematically — implicitly the memes that spread are those with low complexity / high transmissibility (the alpha parameter).

subject: concept:memetic-fitness
quote: Memes are subject to a form of natural selection, which allows them to change over time and even evolve to become more effective replicators.
rationale: Paper explicitly invokes natural selection and fitness as the mechanism by which memes become more effective replicators.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: As defined by Dawkins [1], a meme is any cultural entity that appears to exhibit self-replication.
rationale: Paper grounds its definition of meme in Dawkins' original formulation in The Selfish Gene.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: memes are the cultural analogue of genes
rationale: Paper situates itself squarely in the memetics tradition — cultural transmission as analogous to genetic transmission.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: the social terms, SI and IR, imply a fully connected network. That is, for the social interaction between the susceptible and infected populations, every individual in I has an equal effect on every individual in S and vice versa.
rationale: Paper develops formal network-propagation model — belongs to network epistemology tradition though it makes a strong fully-connected assumption.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: the social terms, SI and IR, imply a fully connected network... In a real society, individuals are distributed by location and cluster in communities... This model does not account for localized sub-populations, and assumes that all individuals have an equal influence on all others.
rationale: Paper assumes fully-connected network (N is a constant), bypassing the N-hop / network-topology factor central to the complexity-ceiling claim — and explicitly flags this as a limitation.

subject: concept:complexity-ceiling
quote: the model can provide a prediction as to at what level the infected population will stabilize, if at all
rationale: Paper formalizes a stable infected-population ceiling determined by network parameters — a mathematical analogue of the complexity ceiling, here as I* = N - gamma/beta.

subject: mechanism:selection
quote: memes do not exist in isolation and their infection and decay parameters would be functions of other memes already present. This makes the parameter values time-dependent... To properly model the infection and decay parameters, the entire memetic ecosystem must be taken into account.
rationale: Paper concedes that transport parameters (alpha, beta, gamma) are themselves shaped by the surrounding memetic ecosystem — i.e. selection pressure from competing memes.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: the infection and decay parameters a, b and c are treated as independent constants. In the real world, memes do not exist in isolation and their infection and decay parameters would be functions of other memes already present.
rationale: Paper's pure-transport SIR formulation is self-acknowledged as inadequate — selection effects (ecosystem competition) re-enter as time-varying parameters.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: Press releases and media reports are major drivers of memes in a society, and as a result can cause successive minor epidemics.
rationale: Paper observes the model fails when the medium (news cycle, press releases) injects energy into the system — implicitly acknowledging medium-specific dynamics that the pure-SIR model cannot capture.

subject: mechanism:manufactured-injection
quote: the spread of memes depends greatly on publicity received from periodic sources such as on-line news sites and popular bloggers, as well as social networks
rationale: Paper acknowledges that meme spread depends on injection from external publicity sources rather than purely organic transmission — a manufactured-injection signal.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: After hitting the first peak reasonably well, the model levels out and does not provide a good approximation of either subsequent peaks or the low base rate of searches. In order to improve results, it is necessary to reduce the size of the data set so that it only includes one major event.
rationale: Pure-transport model fits only single-spike memes; multi-peak phenomena require treating selection/medium as a parallel mechanism injecting energy, supporting the parallel-mechanisms framing.

subject: stage:meme
quote: The viral memetic model best handles memes featuring one large, smooth spike shortly following their birth, and then a gradual taper.
rationale: Paper's empirical focus is the meme stage — its propagation dynamics after a meme is born.

subject: concept:manipulation-surface
quote: the success of the modelling indicates that memes can indeed be considered to be infectious in nature, which opens up whole new frontiers for the likes of education, marketing, and even politics.
rationale: Paper explicitly notes that successful meme modeling opens manipulation surfaces for marketing and politics — the attack-surface dimension of memetic dynamics.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: In the age of the Internet, memes have the ability to spread around the world in very short periods of time.
rationale: Paper frames its motivation around the Internet collapsing transport cost — making global meme propagation feasible and observable.

subject: case:scientists-say-x
quote: Memetic modelling techniques allow for estimation of public interests and predictions of what the public may find interesting in the future. They can model the acceptance of new ideas, the decline of old ones, and the rapid spikes produced by sensational events that happen around the world.
rationale: Paper describes how sensational-event spikes (the headline dynamic) can be modeled — the 'scientists say X' phenomenon as a modeled object.

subject: source:epidemiological-virality
quote: memes do not exist in isolation and their infection and decay parameters would be functions of other memes already present
rationale: Paper's transmission parameter alpha could in principle encode complexity-driven differences but the paper treats it as memewise-empirical — pressure-testing the idea that complexity alone explains virality.

subject: mechanism:transport
quote: Here parameter a is defined as the transmission rate proportionality constant, which represents the fraction of interactions which result in a susceptible individual becoming infected with a meme.
rationale: Paper operationalizes transport as a transmission-rate parameter alpha — a per-interaction probability of meme transfer. Core transport-stage mathematical formalism.

subject: concept:receiver-budget
quote: Depending on the type of meme, people may lose interest over time, so memes can die out as well.
rationale: Paper's gamma (loss-of-interest) parameter is an indirect proxy for receiver attention budget exhaustion, though not framed in those terms.

subject: question:transport-vs-selection
quote: The viral memetic model proposed here is a simplistic first step in modeling infectious outbreaks of culture and ideas.
rationale: Paper self-identifies as a transport-side first step — implicitly raising the question of what a fuller model (with selection dynamics) would look like.

_note: Paper is a short formal SIR-style model paper. Strong on transport-stage math (alpha/beta/gamma parameters, equilibrium analysis). Self-acknowledged gaps: (1) fully-connected network assumption ignores topology, (2) parameters as constants ignore competing-meme ecosystem, (3) cannot model news-cycle reinjection. Catalog gap: no entry for ecosystem-competition / parameter-coupling between memes, which paper flags as future work.
